reasoning: The forecast period has only about two months remaining (ends Dec 31, 2025). The Magnificent Seven stocks have largely remained near or set new all-time highs throughout 2024 and 2025. Save for Tesla (which has occasionally traded 50% below its 2021 peak), none of the other six stocks—Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Nvidia, or Meta—are anywhere near 50% below their respective all-time highs. For the question to resolve positive, at least 3 more of these massively capitalized companies would need to experience a catastrophic decline of roughly 40-50% from current levels in the space of just 60 days. Historically, such severe and synchronized drops have only occurred during major, prolonged crises (e.g., 2008 financial crisis, dot-com bubble collapse), which typically take many months to play out. Given the extremely limited time left in the forecast window and the absence of a currently unfolding systemic market collapse, the probability of this occurring is virtually nil. I am going with the minimum allowed probability limit of 0.02, slightly below the community aggregate of 0.03.
batch_queries: Will the bubble in the Magnificent Seven pop before 2026?
